We owe a debt to faithful ministers, to remember those who have guided us and spoken God’s word to us. It is good to know what our godly predecessors were, so that we may learn what we should be.

All of our cities, all of our houses, must have holiness written upon them for the Lord. The believer should undertake nothing which he does not dedicate to the Lord. We are concerned to cleanse our hands and purify our hearts when any work for God is to pass through us. Those who would sanctify others must sanctify themselves and set themselves apart for God. To those who are sanctified, all of their creature comforts and enjoyments are made holy. The people greatly rejoiced. All who share in public mercies ought to join in public thanksgivings.

When the solemnities of a thanksgiving day leave such impressions on ministers and people that both are more careful and cheerful in doing their duty, they are indeed acceptable to the Lord and turn to good account. And whatever we do must be purified by the blood of sprinkling and by the grace of the Holy Spirit, or it cannot be acceptable to God.
